what_it_does: A git diff patch that adds a top-level view switch to the canvas frontend (canvas/app/src/App.tsx) toggling between the tldraw 'canvas' workspace and a new 'review' workspace, and extends the Vite dev proxy (vite.config.ts) to forward /mockups and /design-system.css to the API target.
observations: Imports a new Review region component and renders it conditionally when view==='review'.
Introduces useState<'canvas'|'review'>('canvas') as the single source of truth for which workspace renders.
Adds a 'view-switch' chip with two buttons (canvas / review) carrying data-ui-ref="ui://view-switch".
Hides the .app-shell via inline style display:none when in review mode rather than unmounting it.
Wraps the Review render in a PanelErrorBoundary named 'review'.
Vite proxy now forwards /api, /mockups, and /design-system.css all to VITE_API_TARGET (default http://localhost:8770).
Heavy inline comments explain the design intent, attributing decisions to Tim with a date (2026-06-08).
standouts: Both workspaces share the SAME AppContext.Provider so the right-hand-man machinery is identical across views.
Deliberately 'no router, just a mode + a switch chip' — minimal structural addition.
The tldraw board keeps running underneath both views; review just covers it.
conventions: Follows the codebase pattern of data-ui-ref addressable element IDs (ui://view-switch).
Follows the PanelErrorBoundary wrapping convention used elsewhere in the shell.
Comments cite Tim quotes and dates as decision provenance — a convention visible in this repo.
concerns: Hiding .app-shell with inline display:none rather than conditional render keeps the canvas chrome mounted; could carry hidden state/cost (file-local observation only).
Proxy entries duplicate the same VITE_API_TARGET expression three times rather than sharing a constant.
notes: A surgical patch adding a canvas/review mode toggle to the frontend shell plus dev-proxy routes for serving the mockup review corpus same-origin.
questions: What does the Review region component actually render, since it is imported but not defined here?
What serves /mockups and /design-system.css on the API target (port 8770)?
purpose_vs_actual: n/a (a patch file with no separate stated purpose; the inline comments match the diff's actual changes)
intention_signals: Trying to add a second workspace (design-mockup review with the right-hand-man) alongside the operating canvas without forking the app's brain — reuse the same AppContext so review and canvas are the same system in two modes.
novelty: The pattern of toggling whole workspaces inside one shared context provider while keeping the tldraw board live underneath, with addressable switch chip.
contribution: Contributes the canvas↔review view-switch mechanism and the dev-proxy wiring that lets a mockup review workspace stage mockups same-origin through the bridge.
summary_for_embedding: A git patch adding a canvas/review view-switch to the frontend App shell (shared AppContext) and extending the Vite dev proxy to serve /mockups and /design-system.css.
intention_for_embedding: To give the canvas app a separate design-mockup review workspace driven by the same right-hand-man machinery, reachable by an in-shell toggle.
